DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE MOTION FOR AN OPPORTUNITY TO RE-DEPOSE PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. MR. SAGE'S DECLARATION THAT ANY ASBESTOS IN DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS WOULD HAVE BEEN ENCAPSULATED AND BOUND BY RESINS, ASPHALTS AND PLASTICIZERS IS TOO VAGUE AND UNCERTAIN IN ITS APPLICATION TO THE FACTS OF THIS CASE TO NEGATE THE ELEMENT OF ANY CLAIM. AS TO THE CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES, DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY PRESENTING PLAINTIFF'S FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE FACT.
